content.content:
71	==== Capture Composition Contract (rblds_ spine)
72	
73	This section governs the host-composed `projects.builds.create` path for Lode
74	captures — distinct from the trigger-autodetect path the Decision above covers.
75	A Lode capture is composed on the host by the capture-assembly spine
76	(`rblds_Spine.sh`) from two data inputs handed by a per-kind body (e.g. bole's
77	`zrbld_ensconce_submit`): an ordered _recipe_ of step rows and an opaque
78	_substitutions_ blob. The spine carries no per-kind branch and no
79	kind-registration mechanism — a new Lode kind is a new body file the spine
80	consumes as data, never a conditional inside the spine.
81	
82	===== Spine/body boundary
83	
84	[cols="1,3",options="header"]
85	|===
86	|Owner |Carries
87	
88	|Spine (`rblds_`)
89	|The step-composition loop (recipe rows -> `steps[]`); the Build-resource
90	envelope shape (`serviceAccount`, `options.automapSubstitutions`,
91	`options.logging`, `options.pool`, `timeout`); submit + build-id persist +
92	console link; the poll via `zrbfc_wait_build_completion`; and the
93	`buildStepOutputs[N]` base64-decode. The capture-domain constants — the mason
94	capture SA, the `TETHER` pool, the regime build timeout — are spine-owned,
95	because every Lode capture fetches upstream bytes as mason over the network.
96	
97	|Body (`rbld«kind»_`)
98	|The recipe rows (pre-resolved); the substitutions blob; the poll ceiling (the
99	one kind-flavored build knob); and the interpretation of the decoded step
100	output (the per-member envelope fan-out).
101	|===
102	
103	The only kind-flavored build knob the body passes is the poll ceiling (ensconce
104	borrows the enshrine ceiling). `serviceAccount`/`pool`/`timeout` are NOT body
105	parameters: they are capture-domain invariants the spine owns. A future kind
106	that needed a different pool or SA would change the spine's capture-domain
107	constants deliberately, not slip past as recipe data.
108	
109	===== Recipe row format
110	
111	A recipe is an ordered list of `|`-delimited step rows. The `|` delimiter is
112	load-bearing: builder image refs carry colons (tags and `sha256:` digests), so a
113	colon delimiter would be ambiguous.
114	
115	----
116	script_path | builder_image | id | entrypoint
117	----
118	
119	[cols="1,3",options="header"]
120	|===
121	|Field |Meaning
122	
123	|`script_path`   |Absolute path to the step script, _pre-resolved by the body_. The spine owns no steps-directory knowledge.
124	|`builder_image` |The step's builder image ref (a reliquary tool image).
125	|`id`            |The Cloud Build step id.
126	|`entrypoint`    |`bash` \| `sh` \| `python3` — selects the composed shebang line. Any other value fails composition.
127	|===
128	
129	The spine reads each script's body (shebang stripped), expands any
130	`#@rbgjs_include` markers against the shared snippet library (see _Composed-snippet
131	library_ below), prepends the shebang the entrypoint selects, and inlines the
132	result as the step's `script` field. This is the same shape the made-side
133	`rbfca_` assemblers use; the capture spine is the canonical single copy for the
134	fetched side.
135	
136	===== Substitutions blob — opaque
137	
138	The body composes the substitutions object (the `_RBGL_*` keys) into a file; the
139	spine slots it verbatim into the Build resource's `substitutions` field and
140	reads no key from it. `options.automapSubstitutions` maps the keys into each
141	step's environment. The spine's opacity to the blob is what keeps it
142	registration-agnostic — a new kind authors a different blob without touching the
143	spine.
144	
145	Invariant (RBSCB): substitutions and `/workspace` carry non-secret data only;
146	secrets are step-local and re-minted.
147	
148	===== Extract contract
149	
150	The spine's extract decodes one step's `buildStepOutputs[N]` slot — base64 to a
151	destination file — where `N` is the only generic parameter. The decoded JSON's
152	shape (for bole: `slot_1`..`slot_N` member envelopes, each a stamp + vouch) is
153	the body's knowledge; the spine is member-count-agnostic. This keeps the
154	N-member envelope axis out of the spine.
155	
156	===== Requires/provides format (designed here; enforced by the recipe validator)
157	
158	The recipe row above is the format implemented today. The capture domain also
159	needs a per-step _requires/provides_ declaration so a dispatch-time validator
160	can reject a bad composition before the expensive build. The FORMAT is fixed
161	here; its enforcement is the recipe-validator pace.
162	
163	A step composes over two register channels:
164	
165	* _Substitution registers_ — `_RBGL_*` keys present in the substitutions blob,
166	  automapped into the step environment.
167	* _/workspace registers_ — non-secret files a step writes for a later step,
168	  named by path relative to `/workspace`.
169	
170	Each step declares two token lists:
171	
172	* `requires` — substitution-register keys and `/workspace` register paths the
173	  step reads before it runs.
174	* `provides` — `/workspace` register paths the step writes (and, for the
175	  terminal capture step, the `buildStepOutputs` slot it authors).
176	
177	The validator walks the recipe in order: every `requires` token must be
178	satisfied by either a key present in the substitutions blob or a `provides`
179	token from an earlier step. An unsatisfiable `requires` fails dispatch with a
180	named-token diagnostic, before submit.
181	
182	The format is deliberately shaped by the capture domain's three axes:
183	
184	* _N-member envelopes_ — the terminal step's `provides` is the single
185	  `buildStepOutputs` slot, not per-member registers; member fan-out is body
186	  knowledge and stays out of the step contract.
187	* _Tool variety_ — registers are declared identically regardless of builder
188	  image or entrypoint, so a `python3` step and a `bash` step speak the same
189	  contract vocabulary.
190	* _Opaque-blob payloads_ — `requires` references substitution keys by NAME; the
191	  validator checks key _presence_, never value shape, preserving the spine's
192	  opacity to the blob.
193	
194	===== Composed-snippet library (rbgjs)
195	
196	Cloud Build offers no native step reuse. Where the same in-step logic recurs
197	across cloud steps, it is authored once as a _snippet_ in the shared cloud-step
198	home `Tools/rbk/rbgjs/` and stitched into each step at host-assembly time. The
199	home deliberately breaks the `rbgj«family»` letter scheme — a shared snippet
200	belongs to no single made-side family, so `s` is the no-family family.
201	
202	*Mechanism.* A step marks an inclusion point with a directive line
203	
204	----
205	#@rbgjs_include «name»
206	----
207	
208	(any leading indentation tolerated). At assembly, `zrbfc_expand_includes`
209	(`rbfcb_BuildHost.sh`, sibling to `zrbfc_write_script_body`, sourced by both the
210	Lode spine and the made-side `rbfca_` vouch assembler) rewrites the step body,
211	splicing in `rbgjs-«name».sh` with its leading shebang stripped. A body with no
212	marker is rewritten unchanged, so an assembler may call the expander for every
213	step. An unknown `«name»` fails the expander, which fails dispatch before submit
214	(crash-fast — no silent skip). The snippet shebang exists only so shellcheck
215	lints the fragment as bash; it never reaches the build.
216	
217	*Register channel — shell vars, not substitutions.* A snippet reads the plain
218	upper-case shell vars the kind sets immediately before the marker, and writes
219	plain shell vars the kind reads after — it is blind to `_RBGx_` substitution
220	names. This is load-bearing: it is precisely what lets one snippet serve callers
221	whose substitution namespaces are disjoint (e.g. Lode's `_RBGL_*` and
222	hallmark-verify's `_RBGV_*`). The substitution-register channel (automapped
223	`_RBGx_` keys) and the snippet shell-var channel are distinct; a snippet never
224	crosses into the substitution channel.
225	
226	*The library (each snippet's requires/provides):*
227	
228	[cols="1,3",options="header"]
229	|===
230	|Snippet |Contract
231	
232	|`token-fetch`        |_requires_ none (ambient metadata server); _provides_ `TOKEN`. The canonical skopeo-to-GAR metadata-server token (RBSCB), in-memory, never to `/workspace`.
233	|`skopeo-fingerprint` |_requires_ `ORIGIN`, `RAW_FILE`; _provides_ `SHA`, `FINGERPRINT`. Inspect the raw manifest, take its canonical sha256, derive the sanitized-origin glance fingerprint.
234	|`buildx-bootstrap`   |_requires_ none; _provides_ the `rb-builder` buildx builder, created-if-absent and selected. Idempotent under retry; run once per step.
235	|`buildx-push`        |_requires_ `PUSH_URI`, `PUSH_PLATFORMS`, `PUSH_CTX` (and the bootstrap builder); _provides_ the image pushed to `PUSH_URI`. The irreducible FROM-scratch push.
236	|===
237	
238	*Share the primitive, not the whole step.* The vouch-push step (Lode `rbgjl02`,
239	hallmark-verify `rbgjv03`) is NOT one shared step: the two diverge on five
240	load-bearing axes — push cardinality (loop-N members vs single), destination-URI
241	template and arity, platform source (literal `linux/amd64` vs a `/workspace`
242	platforms file), a conditional Dockerfile (conjure verify files), and disjoint
243	substitution sets. Only the buildx bootstrap and the push invocation are
244	identical, so only those are shared — as `buildx-bootstrap` + `buildx-push`.
245	Cardinality, URI construction, platform choice, and context assembly (which JSON,

==== Capture Composition Contract (rblds_ spine)

This section governs the host-composed `projects.builds.create` path for Lode
captures — distinct from the trigger-autodetect path the Decision above covers.
A Lode capture is composed on the host by the capture-assembly spine
(`rblds_Spine.sh`) from two data inputs handed by a per-kind body (e.g. bole's
`zrbld_ensconce_submit`): an ordered _recipe_ of step rows and an opaque
_substitutions_ blob. The spine carries no per-kind branch and no
kind-registration mechanism — a new Lode kind is a new body file the spine
consumes as data, never a conditional inside the spine.

===== Spine/body boundary

[cols="1,3",options="header"]
|===
|Owner |Carries

|Spine (`rblds_`)
|The step-composition loop (recipe rows -> `steps[]`); the Build-resource
envelope shape (`serviceAccount`, `options.automapSubstitutions`,
`options.logging`, `options.pool`, `timeout`); submit + build-id persist +
console link; the poll via `zrbfc_wait_build_completion`; and the
`buildStepOutputs[N]` base64-decode. The capture-domain constants — the mason
capture SA, the `TETHER` pool, the regime build timeout — are spine-owned,
because every Lode capture fetches upstream bytes as mason over the network.

|Body (`rbld«kind»_`)
|The recipe rows (pre-resolved); the substitutions blob; the poll ceiling (the
one kind-flavored build knob); and the interpretation of the decoded step
output (the per-member envelope fan-out).
|===

The only kind-flavored build knob the body passes is the poll ceiling (ensconce
borrows the enshrine ceiling). `serviceAccount`/`pool`/`timeout` are NOT body
parameters: they are capture-domain invariants the spine owns. A future kind
that needed a different pool or SA would change the spine's capture-domain
constants deliberately, not slip past as recipe data.

===== Recipe row format

A recipe is an ordered list of `|`-delimited step rows. The `|` delimiter is
load-bearing: builder image refs carry colons (tags and `sha256:` digests), so a
colon delimiter would be ambiguous.

----
script_path | builder_image | id | entrypoint
----

[cols="1,3",options="header"]
|===
|Field |Meaning

|`script_path`   |Absolute path to the step script, _pre-resolved by the body_. The spine owns no steps-directory knowledge.
|`builder_image` |The step's builder image ref (a reliquary tool image).
|`id`            |The Cloud Build step id.
|`entrypoint`    |`bash` \| `sh` \| `python3` — selects the composed shebang line. Any other value fails composition.
|===

The spine reads each script's body (shebang stripped), expands any
`#@rbgjs_include` markers against the shared snippet library (see _Composed-snippet
library_ below), prepends the shebang the entrypoint selects, and inlines the
result as the step's `script` field. This is the same shape the made-side
`rbfca_` assemblers use; the capture spine is the canonical single copy for the
fetched side.

===== Substitutions blob — opaque

The body composes the substitutions object (the `_RBGL_*` keys) into a file; the
spine slots it verbatim into the Build resource's `substitutions` field and
reads no key from it. `options.automapSubstitutions` maps the keys into each
step's environment. The spine's opacity to the blob is what keeps it
registration-agnostic — a new kind authors a different blob without touching the
spine.

Invariant (RBSCB): substitutions and `/workspace` carry non-secret data only;
secrets are step-local and re-minted.

===== Extract contract

The spine's extract decodes one step's `buildStepOutputs[N]` slot — base64 to a
destination file — where `N` is the only generic parameter. The decoded JSON's
shape (for bole: `slot_1`..`slot_N` member envelopes, each a stamp + vouch) is
the body's knowledge; the spine is member-count-agnostic. This keeps the
N-member envelope axis out of the spine.

===== Requires/provides format (designed here; enforced by the recipe validator)

The recipe row above is the format implemented today. The capture domain also
needs a per-step _requires/provides_ declaration so a dispatch-time validator
can reject a bad composition before the expensive build. The FORMAT is fixed
here; its enforcement is the recipe-validator pace.

A step composes over two register channels:

* _Substitution registers_ — `_RBGL_*` keys present in the substitutions blob,
  automapped into the step environment.
* _/workspace registers_ — non-secret files a step writes for a later step,
  named by path relative to `/workspace`.

Each step declares two token lists:

* `requires` — substitution-register keys and `/workspace` register paths the
  step reads before it runs.
* `provides` — `/workspace` register paths the step writes (and, for the
  terminal capture step, the `buildStepOutputs` slot it authors).

The validator walks the recipe in order: every `requires` token must be
satisfied by either a key present in the substitutions blob or a `provides`
token from an earlier step. An unsatisfiable `requires` fails dispatch with a
named-token diagnostic, before submit.

The format is deliberately shaped by the capture domain's three axes:

* _N-member envelopes_ — the terminal step's `provides` is the single
  `buildStepOutputs` slot, not per-member registers; member fan-out is body
  knowledge and stays out of the step contract.
* _Tool variety_ — registers are declared identically regardless of builder
  image or entrypoint, so a `python3` step and a `bash` step speak the same
  contract vocabulary.
* _Opaque-blob payloads_ — `requires` references substitution keys by NAME; the
  validator checks key _presence_, never value shape, preserving the spine's
  opacity to the blob.

===== Composed-snippet library (rbgjs)

Cloud Build offers no native step reuse. Where the same in-step logic recurs
across cloud steps, it is authored once as a _snippet_ in the shared cloud-step
home `Tools/rbk/rbgjs/` and stitched into each step at host-assembly time. The
home deliberately breaks the `rbgj«family»` letter scheme — a shared snippet
belongs to no single made-side family, so `s` is the no-family family.

*Mechanism.* A step marks an inclusion point with a directive line

----
#@rbgjs_include «name»
----

(any leading indentation tolerated). At assembly, `zrbfc_expand_includes`
(`rbfcb_BuildHost.sh`, sibling to `zrbfc_write_script_body`, sourced by both the
Lode spine and the made-side `rbfca_` vouch assembler) rewrites the step body,
splicing in `rbgjs-«name».sh` with its leading shebang stripped. A body with no
marker is rewritten unchanged, so an assembler may call the expander for every
step. An unknown `«name»` fails the expander, which fails dispatch before submit
(crash-fast — no silent skip). The snippet shebang exists only so shellcheck
lints the fragment as bash; it never reaches the build.

*Register channel — shell vars, not substitutions.* A snippet reads the plain
upper-case shell vars the kind sets immediately before the marker, and writes
plain shell vars the kind reads after — it is blind to `_RBGx_` substitution
names. This is load-bearing: it is precisely what lets one snippet serve callers
whose substitution namespaces are disjoint (e.g. Lode's `_RBGL_*` and
hallmark-verify's `_RBGV_*`). The substitution-register channel (automapped
`_RBGx_` keys) and the snippet shell-var channel are distinct; a snippet never
crosses into the substitution channel.

*The library (each snippet's requires/provides):*

[cols="1,3",options="header"]
|===
|Snippet |Contract

|`token-fetch`        |_requires_ none (ambient metadata server); _provides_ `TOKEN`. The canonical skopeo-to-GAR metadata-server token (RBSCB), in-memory, never to `/workspace`.
|`skopeo-fingerprint` |_requires_ `ORIGIN`, `RAW_FILE`; _provides_ `SHA`, `FINGERPRINT`. Inspect the raw manifest, take its canonical sha256, derive the sanitized-origin glance fingerprint.
|`buildx-bootstrap`   |_requires_ none; _provides_ the `rb-builder` buildx builder, created-if-absent and selected. Idempotent under retry; run once per step.
|`buildx-push`        |_requires_ `PUSH_URI`, `PUSH_PLATFORMS`, `PUSH_CTX` (and the bootstrap builder); _provides_ the image pushed to `PUSH_URI`. The irreducible FROM-scratch push.
|===

*Share the primitive, not the whole step.* The vouch-push step (Lode `rbgjl02`,
hallmark-verify `rbgjv03`) is NOT one shared step: the two diverge on five
load-bearing axes — push cardinality (loop-N members vs single), destination-URI
template and arity, platform source (literal `linux/amd64` vs a `/workspace`
platforms file), a conditional Dockerfile (conjure verify files), and disjoint
substitution sets. Only the buildx bootstrap and the push invocation are
identical, so only those are shared — as `buildx-bootstrap` + `buildx-push`.
Cardinality, URI construction, platform choice, and context assembly (which JSON,

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at

9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Lode - bole body (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbldk_):
20	#   ensconce — capture an upstream base image into a Lode (Director credentials)
21	# The bole rides the capture-assembly spine (rblds_): this body owns only the
22	# kind-specific data — the ensconce recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch push),
23	# the substitutions blob, and the per-member envelope extract loop — and composes
24	# them through zrbld_spine_dispatch / zrbld_spine_extract. No build-submission or
25	# step-composition machinery lives here.
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	# Capture-file extension for buf_write_fact_multi (filesystem-as-data-bus).
30	# Pure literal tinder — one provenance fact-file per captured Lode, keyed by
31	# stamp: "<stamp>.${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}". Mirrors the roster fact precedent.
32	
33	######################################################################
34	# Internal Helpers (zrbld_*)
35	
36	# Internal: compose the ensconce capture recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch
37	# push) and its substitutions blob, then ride the capture spine to submit and
38	# poll. The spine owns the capture-domain build knobs (mason SA, TETHER pool,
39	# regime timeout); this body chooses only the recipe, the substitutions, and the
40	# enshrine-borrowed poll ceiling.
41	# Args: token origin stamp
42	zrbld_ensconce_submit() {
43	  zrbld_sentinel
44	
45	  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
46	  local -r z_origin="${2:?Origin required}"
47	  local -r z_stamp="${3:?Stamp required}"
48	
49	  buc_step "Constructing ensconce capture recipe"
50	  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
51	  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
52	
53	  # Recipe rows: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint, pre-resolved for the
54	  # spine. The | delimiter is load-bearing — builder refs carry colons (tags).
55	  local -r z_recipe=(
56	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_skopeo}|ensconce-capture|bash"
57	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|assemble-push-vouch|bash"
58	  )
59	
60	  buc_log_args "Composing ensconce substitutions blob"
61	  local -r z_subs_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}subs.json"
62	  jq -n \
63	    --arg zjq_gar_host     "${z_gar_host}" \
64	    --arg zjq_gar_path     "${z_gar_path}" \
65	    --arg zjq_lodes_root   "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}" \
66	    --arg zjq_tag_bole     "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE}" \
67	    --arg zjq_tag_vouch    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
68	    --arg zjq_tag_digest   "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}" \
69	    --arg zjq_trust_grade  "${RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED}" \
70	    --arg zjq_vouch_schema "${RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA}" \
71	    --arg zjq_acquired_by  "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" \
72	    --arg zjq_origin_1     "${z_origin}" \
73	    --arg zjq_stamp_1      "${z_stamp}" \
74	    '{
75	      _RBGL_GAR_HOST:          $zjq_gar_host,
76	      _RBGL_GAR_PATH:          $zjq_gar_path,
77	      _RBGL_LODES_ROOT:        $zjq_lodes_root,
78	      _RBGL_TAG_BOLE:          $zjq_tag_bole,
79	      _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH:         $zjq_tag_vouch,
80	      _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX: $zjq_tag_digest,
81	      _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE:       $zjq_trust_grade,
82	      _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA:      $zjq_vouch_schema,
83	      _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY:       $zjq_acquired_by,
84	      _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN:    $zjq_origin_1,
85	      _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN:    "",
86	      _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN:    "",
87	      _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP:      $zjq_stamp_1,
88	      _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP:      "",
89	      _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP:      ""
90	    }' > "${z_subs_file}" \
91	    || buc_die "Failed to compose ensconce substitutions blob"
92	
93	  zrbld_spine_dispatch \
94	    "${z_token}" "Ensconce" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_ENSHRINE}" \
95	    "${z_subs_file}" "${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}" \
96	    "${z_recipe[@]}"
97	}
98	
99	# Internal: extract per-Lode provenance envelopes from the completed ensconce
100	# build and write one capture-file per Lode member. The skopeo capture step
101	# (step 0) authors the base64 JSON; the docker vouch step writes no output. The
102	# spine decodes step 0's slot to a file; the per-member slot_n envelope below is
103	# bole's shape, not the spine's.
104	zrbld_ensconce_extract() {
105	  zrbld_sentinel
106	
107	  buc_step "Extracting capture results from build step outputs"
108	
109	  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}output.json"
110	  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"
111	
112	  buc_log_args "Ensconce output:"
113	  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"
114	
115	  local z_n=""
116	  local z_slot_key=""
117	  local z_stamp_file=""
118	  local z_stamp=""
119	  local z_vouch_file=""
120	  local z_vouch=""
121	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
122	    z_slot_key="slot_${z_n}"
123	    z_stamp_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}${z_n}_stamp.txt"
124	    jq -r ".${z_slot_key}.stamp // empty" "${z_output_file}" > "${z_stamp_file}" \
125	      || buc_die "Failed to read stamp for ${z_slot_key}"
126	    z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
127	    test -n "${z_stamp}" || continue
128	
129	    z_vouch_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}${z_n}_vouch.json"
130	    jq -c ".${z_slot_key}.vouch" "${z_output_file}" > "${z_vouch_file}" \
131	      || buc_die "Failed to extract vouch envelope for ${z_slot_key}"
132	    z_vouch=$(<"${z_vouch_file}")
133	    test -n "${z_vouch}" || buc_die "Empty vouch envelope for ${z_slot_key}"
134	
135	    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_stamp}" "${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}" "${z_vouch}"
136	    buc_success "Ensconced Lode ${z_stamp} — capture-file ${z_stamp}.${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}"
137	  done
138	}
139	
140	######################################################################
141	# External Functions (rbld_*)
142	
143	rbld_ensconce() {
144	  zrbld_sentinel
145	
146	  buc_doc_brief "Ensconce an upstream base image into a Lode (parallel rbi_ld capture)"
147	  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory declaring the base ORIGIN"
148	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
149	
150	  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) and load.
151	  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
152	  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
153	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
154	  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
155	
156	  # Resolve the single base ORIGIN. Base-kind ensconce captures one base per
157	  # Lode per invocation; multi-base vessels are dispatched per base. Every real
158	  # vessel declares exactly one base slot today.
159	  local z_origin=""
160	  local z_origin_count=0
161	  local z_n=""
162	  local z_origin_var=""
163	  local z_slot_origin=""
164	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
165	    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
166	    z_slot_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
167	    test -n "${z_slot_origin}" || continue
168	    z_origin="${z_slot_origin}"
169	    z_origin_count=$((z_origin_count + 1))
170	  done
171	
172	  test "${z_origin_count}" -ne 0 \
173	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no upstream base-image slot (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN)"
174	  test "${z_origin_count}" -eq 1 \
175	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares ${z_origin_count} base slots; base-kind ensconce captures one base per Lode — invoke per base"
176	
177	  # Reject producer-vessel pins: an origin naming a local vessel directory is a
178	  # made-side hallmark-pin (bind/airgap), not an upstream base to capture.
179	  test ! -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_origin}" \
180	    || buc_die "Origin '${z_origin}' names a producer vessel — base-Lode capture is for upstream bases, not hallmark-pins"
181	
182	  buc_info "Ensconce base: ${z_origin}"
183	
184	  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (skopeo for capture, docker for vouch).
185	  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images
186	
187	  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
188	  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"
189	
190	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
191	  local z_token=""
192	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
193	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
194	
195	  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
196	  # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
197	  local -r z_stamp="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
198	  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
199	
200	  zrbld_ensconce_submit "${z_token}" "${z_origin}" "${z_stamp}"
201	  zrbld_ensconce_extract
202	
203	  buc_success "Ensconce complete: ${z_origin} -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
204	}
205	
206	# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Lode - bole body (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbldk_):
#   ensconce — capture an upstream base image into a Lode (Director credentials)
# The bole rides the capture-assembly spine (rblds_): this body owns only the
# kind-specific data — the ensconce recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch push),
# the substitutions blob, and the per-member envelope extract loop — and composes
# them through zrbld_spine_dispatch / zrbld_spine_extract. No build-submission or
# step-composition machinery lives here.

set -euo pipefail

# Capture-file extension for buf_write_fact_multi (filesystem-as-data-bus).
# Pure literal tinder — one provenance fact-file per captured Lode, keyed by
# stamp: "<stamp>.${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}". Mirrors the roster fact precedent.

######################################################################
# Internal Helpers (zrbld_*)

# Internal: compose the ensconce capture recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch
# push) and its substitutions blob, then ride the capture spine to submit and
# poll. The spine owns the capture-domain build knobs (mason SA, TETHER pool,
# regime timeout); this body chooses only the recipe, the substitutions, and the
# enshrine-borrowed poll ceiling.
# Args: token origin stamp
zrbld_ensconce_submit() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
  local -r z_origin="${2:?Origin required}"
  local -r z_stamp="${3:?Stamp required}"

  buc_step "Constructing ensconce capture recipe"
  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"

  # Recipe rows: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint, pre-resolved for the
  # spine. The | delimiter is load-bearing — builder refs carry colons (tags).
  local -r z_recipe=(
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_skopeo}|ensconce-capture|bash"
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|assemble-push-vouch|bash"
  )

  buc_log_args "Composing ensconce substitutions blob"
  local -r z_subs_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}subs.json"
  jq -n \
    --arg zjq_gar_host     "${z_gar_host}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_path     "${z_gar_path}" \
    --arg zjq_lodes_root   "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_bole     "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_vouch    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_digest   "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}" \
    --arg zjq_trust_grade  "${RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED}" \
    --arg zjq_vouch_schema "${RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA}" \
    --arg zjq_acquired_by  "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" \
    --arg zjq_origin_1     "${z_origin}" \
    --arg zjq_stamp_1      "${z_stamp}" \
    '{
      _RBGL_GAR_HOST:          $zjq_gar_host,
      _RBGL_GAR_PATH:          $zjq_gar_path,
      _RBGL_LODES_ROOT:        $zjq_lodes_root,
      _RBGL_TAG_BOLE:          $zjq_tag_bole,
      _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH:         $zjq_tag_vouch,
      _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX: $zjq_tag_digest,
      _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE:       $zjq_trust_grade,
      _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA:      $zjq_vouch_schema,
      _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY:       $zjq_acquired_by,
      _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN:    $zjq_origin_1,
      _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN:    "",
      _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN:    "",
      _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP:      $zjq_stamp_1,
      _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP:      "",
      _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP:      ""
    }' > "${z_subs_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to compose ensconce substitutions blob"

  zrbld_spine_dispatch \
    "${z_token}" "Ensconce" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_ENSHRINE}" \
    "${z_subs_file}" "${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}" \
    "${z_recipe[@]}"
}

# Internal: extract per-Lode provenance envelopes from the completed ensconce
# build and write one capture-file per Lode member. The skopeo capture step
# (step 0) authors the base64 JSON; the docker vouch step writes no output. The
# spine decodes step 0's slot to a file; the per-member slot_n envelope below is
# bole's shape, not the spine's.
zrbld_ensconce_extract() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  buc_step "Extracting capture results from build step outputs"

  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}output.json"
  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"

  buc_log_args "Ensconce output:"
  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"

  local z_n=""
  local z_slot_key=""
  local z_stamp_file=""
  local z_stamp=""
  local z_vouch_file=""
  local z_vouch=""
  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_slot_key="slot_${z_n}"
    z_stamp_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}${z_n}_stamp.txt"
    jq -r ".${z_slot_key}.stamp // empty" "${z_output_file}" > "${z_stamp_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to read stamp for ${z_slot_key}"
    z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
    test -n "${z_stamp}" || continue

    z_vouch_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}${z_n}_vouch.json"
    jq -c ".${z_slot_key}.vouch" "${z_output_file}" > "${z_vouch_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to extract vouch envelope for ${z_slot_key}"
    z_vouch=$(<"${z_vouch_file}")
    test -n "${z_vouch}" || buc_die "Empty vouch envelope for ${z_slot_key}"

    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_stamp}" "${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}" "${z_vouch}"
    buc_success "Ensconced Lode ${z_stamp} — capture-file ${z_stamp}.${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}"
  done
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbld_*)

rbld_ensconce() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Ensconce an upstream base image into a Lode (parallel rbi_ld capture)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory declaring the base ORIGIN"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) and load.
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Resolve the single base ORIGIN. Base-kind ensconce captures one base per
  # Lode per invocation; multi-base vessels are dispatched per base. Every real
  # vessel declares exactly one base slot today.
  local z_origin=""
  local z_origin_count=0
  local z_n=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  local z_slot_origin=""
  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_slot_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    test -n "${z_slot_origin}" || continue
    z_origin="${z_slot_origin}"
    z_origin_count=$((z_origin_count + 1))
  done

  test "${z_origin_count}" -ne 0 \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no upstream base-image slot (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN)"
  test "${z_origin_count}" -eq 1 \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares ${z_origin_count} base slots; base-kind ensconce captures one base per Lode — invoke per base"

  # Reject producer-vessel pins: an origin naming a local vessel directory is a
  # made-side hallmark-pin (bind/airgap), not an upstream base to capture.
  test ! -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_origin}" \
    || buc_die "Origin '${z_origin}' names a producer vessel — base-Lode capture is for upstream bases, not hallmark-pins"

  buc_info "Ensconce base: ${z_origin}"

  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (skopeo for capture, docker for vouch).
  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images

  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
  # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
  local -r z_stamp="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"

  zrbld_ensconce_submit "${z_token}" "${z_origin}" "${z_stamp}"
  zrbld_ensconce_extract

  buc_success "Ensconce complete: ${z_origin} -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
}

# eof

content.text:
I've read the substrate. Here's the mount for **₢BXAAF — dispatch-recipe-validator**.

## Goal (one sentence)
Add a dispatch-time gate that walks a Lode capture recipe in order and, for each step, confirms every declared `requires` token is already satisfied — by a key in the substitutions blob or a `provides` from an earlier step — failing a bad composition with a named-token diagnostic *before* the ~100-minute build is submitted.

## Heat context
- **₣BX** — rbk-09-capture-machinery-unification (racing, 7/11). A behavior-*preserving* refactor heat; this pace is its **single declared behavior-adding sliver**.
- Recent work stood up the substrate this pace gates: the data-driven spine (`zrbld_spine_dispatch`, ₢BXAAD) and the shared snippet library (₢BXAAE). The contract this validator enforces is already written into RBSCJ "Capture Composition Contract."

## Docket (it's marked **[rough]**)
Character: new code with its own tests. Done = a bad composition fails at dispatch with a clear message; every existing recipe passes unchanged; unit tests cover the reject-path. Cinched: accept-path leaves all recipes unchanged; reject-path has no fixture, so it carries targeted unit tests.

**Name assessment:** `dispatch-recipe-validator` fits the docket cleanly — no relabel needed.

## What I found in the substrate (and the one real open decision)
The contract (RBSCJ §"Requires/provides format") fixes the **semantics and the walk** precisely: two token lists per step over two channels — substitution-register keys (`_RBGL_*`) and `/workspace` paths — and the in-order satisfaction walk. What it deliberately leaves to *this pace* is the **carrier**: today a recipe row is a 4-tuple (`script_path|builder|id|entrypoint`) and carries no requires/provides anywhere. So I have to decide where a step *declares* them.

Two correctness points I want to honor:
- The validator's channel is **substitutions + /workspace**, and must stay **blind to the snippet shell-var channel** (`TOKEN`, `ORIGIN`, `PUSH_URI`…). The contract is emphatic that those never cross into the substitution channel — the expander already crash-fails on a bad `#@rbgjs_include`, so snippet vars are step-internal and out of scope here.
- `rbldv_` is **reserved for the future podvm kind**, so the validator should live as a **spine function** (`zrbld_spine_validate`, called inside `zrbld_spine_dispatch` right before `rbuh_json POST`), not a new `rbld«letter»_` file. That keeps it in `rblds_` and sidesteps the kind-letter.

## Proposed approach
1. **Declare requires/provides in each step-script header** (recommended carrier — see question below), reusing the snippet header grammar the codebase already has. This makes a step's contract intrinsic to the step (it doesn't vary by recipe), avoids re-declaring at each recipe site, and feeds the CBG capstone (self-documenting steps).
2. **`zrbld_spine_validate`** in `rblds_Spine.sh`: seed a satisfied-set from `jq -r 'keys[]'` of the subs blob, then walk recipe rows in order — each row's `requires ⊆ satisfied`, else `buc_die` with the offending step id + token; on pass, union its `provides` into the set. Call it from `zrbld_spine_dispatch` before submit.
3. **Theurge unit tests** for the reject-path (unsatisfiable require, out-of-order provide) plus an accept-path regression that the bole recipe still passes. This is the no-fixture reject-path the docket calls out.
4. **Refresh RBSCJ** to mark the format as now-enforced (the §156 note currently says "enforced by the recipe validator" in future tense).

**Execution strategy:** sequential, opus-tier — single new spine function + small wiring + step-header additions + targeted unit tests. Design-judgment work, not mechanical. Inner loop is shellcheck + theurge unit tests (no cook needed — this is the cheap-guard pace; the full skirmish is the heat gate, a later pace).

The one fork worth your call before I build:

questions.question: Where should each step declare its requires/provides token lists? (The RBSCJ contract fixes the semantics + walk; the carrier is this pace's call.)

options.label: Step-script header (recommended)
options.description: Add `#@requires`/`#@provides` directive lines to each Lode step script (rbgjl01/rbgjl02), reusing the snippet header precedent. The contract lives WITH the code that reads the vars — intrinsic to the step, declared once, not re-stated per recipe. Validator reads each script_path's header (spine already reads the body). Serves the CBG capstone (self-documenting steps). Cost: a small comment-grammar parse + headers added to the step files.
options.preview:
rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh:
  #!/bin/bash
  #@requires _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN _RBGL_GAR_HOST
  #@provides /workspace/capture.json
  ...

spine walk:
  satisfied = keys(subs_blob)
  for row in recipe:
    assert row.requires <= satisfied  # else buc_die
    satisfied |= row.provides

options.label: Extend the recipe row
options.description: Widen the recipe tuple to `script_path|builder|id|entrypoint|requires|provides` (token lists as extra |-fields). Declaration lives in the body's recipe array alongside the subs blob. Keeps everything the validator walks in one structure; no script-header parse. Cost: widens the row format the spine parses, and re-states a step's intrinsic contract at every recipe that uses it (drift risk if a step is reused).
options.preview:
rbldb_Bole.sh recipe:
  z_recipe=(
   "...rbgjl01.sh|$skopeo|ensconce-capture|bash|_RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN,_RBGL_GAR_HOST|/workspace/capture.json"
   "...rbgjl02.sh|$docker|assemble-push-vouch|bash|/workspace/capture.json|"
  )

spine parses 6 fields, walks requires/provides fields 5 & 6

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 01: Ensconce a base image into a Lode (capture) via skopeo
3	# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
4	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT,
5	#                _RBGL_TAG_BOLE, _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX,
6	#                _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA, _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY,
7	#                _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN,
8	#                _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP
9	#
10	# For each non-empty (ORIGIN, STAMP) slot: inspect upstream, measure the canonical
11	# digest, copy --all into ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp>, then apply the member
12	# tags by GAR->GAR retag (dedups blobs). Author the provenance envelope and stage
13	# it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
14	# /builder/outputs/output. Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server.
15	#
16	# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>            (one package = one Lode)
17	# Member tags on that package, all pointing at the base manifest:
18	#   :<TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX><full-hex>   canonical OCI digest (exact cross-Lode dedup)
19	#   :<TAG_BOLE>                      uniform greppable handle
20	#   :<sanitized-origin>-<sha10>      UNSPRUED — name + glance-fingerprint (= legacy enshrine anchor)
21	# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
22	
23	set -euo pipefail
24	echo "=== Ensconce base images into Lodes ==="
25	
26	# Obtain OAuth2 token from metadata server (Mason SA) — shared library snippet.
27	#@rbgjs_include token-fetch
28	
29	# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
30	# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
31	SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
32	SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
33	SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"
34	SLOT_1_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP}"
35	SLOT_2_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP}"
36	SLOT_3_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP}"
37	
38	# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole build.
39	ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
40	
41	# Stamp roster for step 02 (one line per captured Lode).
42	: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
43	
44	# Initialize result JSON (host reads this back as the capture-file source)
45	RESULT='{'
46	FIRST=true
47	
48	for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
49	  case "${SLOT}" in
50	    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_1_STAMP}" ;;
51	    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_2_STAMP}" ;;
52	    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_3_STAMP}" ;;
53	  esac
54	  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue
55	  test -n "${STAMP}"  || { echo "FATAL: slot ${SLOT} has ORIGIN but no STAMP" >&2; exit 1; }
56	
57	  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} -> ${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP} ---"
58	
59	  # Inspect upstream, take the canonical digest, derive the glance fingerprint —
60	  # shared library snippet (requires ORIGIN + RAW_FILE; provides SHA + FINGERPRINT).
61	  RAW_FILE="/workspace/ensconce_raw_${SLOT}.json"
62	#@rbgjs_include skopeo-fingerprint
63	
64	  DIGEST_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}${SHA}"
65	  PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
66	
67	  echo "Package: ${PKG}"
68	  echo "Digest:  sha256:${SHA}"
69	  echo "Tags:    ${DIGEST_TAG}, ${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}, ${FINGERPRINT}"
70	
71	  # Copy upstream into the Lode package under the canonical digest tag.
72	  skopeo copy --all \
73	    "docker://${ORIGIN}" \
74	    "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
75	    --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
76	    || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
77	
78	  # Apply remaining member tags by GAR->GAR retag (same blobs, manifest re-tag only).
79	  for MEMBER_TAG in "${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" "${FINGERPRINT}"; do
80	    skopeo copy --all \
81	      "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
82	      "docker://${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}" \
83	      --src-creds  "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
84	      --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
85	      || { echo "FATAL: skopeo retag ${MEMBER_TAG} failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
86	  done
87	
88	  echo "Slot ${SLOT} ensconced: ${STAMP}"
89	
90	  # Author the provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and the
91	  # host capture-file). No jq dependency — values are controlled (sanitized origin,
92	  # hex digest, SA email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
93	  # members[] is the cardinality axis — length 1 for the bole singleton.
94	  ENVELOPE='{'
95	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
96	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"bole\","
97	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
98	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
99	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
100	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
101	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
102	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
103	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[{"
104	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"name\":\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\","
105	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\","
106	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\","
107	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"verification\":\"oci-digest\","
108	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"tags\":[\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\",\"${DIGEST_TAG}\",\"${FINGERPRINT}\"]"
109	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}]}"
110	
111	  # Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact).
112	  printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
113	  echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
114	
115	  # Accumulate host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope).
116	  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
117	    FIRST=false
118	  else
119	    RESULT="${RESULT},"
120	  fi
121	  RESULT="${RESULT}\"slot_${SLOT}\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}"
122	done
123	
124	RESULT="${RESULT}}"
125	
126	echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
127	echo "${RESULT}"
128	
129	# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts per-Lode envelope -> capture-file)
130	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
131	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
132	
133	echo "=== Ensconce capture step complete ==="
134

#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 01: Ensconce a base image into a Lode (capture) via skopeo
# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT,
#                _RBGL_TAG_BOLE, _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX,
#                _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA, _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY,
#                _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN,
#                _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP
#
# For each non-empty (ORIGIN, STAMP) slot: inspect upstream, measure the canonical
# digest, copy --all into ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp>, then apply the member
# tags by GAR->GAR retag (dedups blobs). Author the provenance envelope and stage
# it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
# /builder/outputs/output. Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server.
#
# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>            (one package = one Lode)
# Member tags on that package, all pointing at the base manifest:
#   :<TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX><full-hex>   canonical OCI digest (exact cross-Lode dedup)
#   :<TAG_BOLE>                      uniform greppable handle
#   :<sanitized-origin>-<sha10>      UNSPRUED — name + glance-fingerprint (= legacy enshrine anchor)
# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Ensconce base images into Lodes ==="

# Obtain OAuth2 token from metadata server (Mason SA) — shared library snippet.
#@rbgjs_include token-fetch

# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_1_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP}"
SLOT_2_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP}"
SLOT_3_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP}"

# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole build.
ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

# Stamp roster for step 02 (one line per captured Lode).
: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt

# Initialize result JSON (host reads this back as the capture-file source)
RESULT='{'
FIRST=true

for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
  case "${SLOT}" in
    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_1_STAMP}" ;;
    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_2_STAMP}" ;;
    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_3_STAMP}" ;;
  esac
  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue
  test -n "${STAMP}"  || { echo "FATAL: slot ${SLOT} has ORIGIN but no STAMP" >&2; exit 1; }

  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} -> ${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP} ---"

  # Inspect upstream, take the canonical digest, derive the glance fingerprint —
  # shared library snippet (requires ORIGIN + RAW_FILE; provides SHA + FINGERPRINT).
  RAW_FILE="/workspace/ensconce_raw_${SLOT}.json"
#@rbgjs_include skopeo-fingerprint

  DIGEST_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}${SHA}"
  PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"

  echo "Package: ${PKG}"
  echo "Digest:  sha256:${SHA}"
  echo "Tags:    ${DIGEST_TAG}, ${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}, ${FINGERPRINT}"

  # Copy upstream into the Lode package under the canonical digest tag.
  skopeo copy --all \
    "docker://${ORIGIN}" \
    "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
    --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Apply remaining member tags by GAR->GAR retag (same blobs, manifest re-tag only).
  for MEMBER_TAG in "${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" "${FINGERPRINT}"; do
    skopeo copy --all \
      "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
      "docker://${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}" \
      --src-creds  "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
      --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
      || { echo "FATAL: skopeo retag ${MEMBER_TAG} failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
  done

  echo "Slot ${SLOT} ensconced: ${STAMP}"

  # Author the provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and the
  # host capture-file). No jq dependency — values are controlled (sanitized origin,
  # hex digest, SA email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
  # members[] is the cardinality axis — length 1 for the bole singleton.
  ENVELOPE='{'
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"bole\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[{"
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"name\":\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"verification\":\"oci-digest\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"tags\":[\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\",\"${DIGEST_TAG}\",\"${FINGERPRINT}\"]"
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}]}"

  # Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact).
  printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
  echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt

  # Accumulate host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope).
  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
    FIRST=false
  else
    RESULT="${RESULT},"
  fi
  RESULT="${RESULT}\"slot_${SLOT}\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}"
done

RESULT="${RESULT}}"

echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
echo "${RESULT}"

# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts per-Lode envelope -> capture-file)
mkdir -p /builder/outputs
printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output

echo "=== Ensconce capture step complete ==="

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 02: Push each Lode's provenance envelope as its :rbi_vouch artifact
3	# Builder: docker (from reliquary)
4	# Entrypoint: bash
5	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH
6	#
7	# Note: The Dockerfile echo-sequence below is intentional — this script runs
8	# inside a Cloud Build container, not under BCG module discipline.

12	# /workspace/lode_stamps.txt. Each envelope rides into the SAME package as the
13	# base manifest, under the :rbi_vouch tag — a distinct manifest, one per Lode.
14	# Vouch content is architecture-independent; single-platform is sufficient.

17	
18	echo "=== Assemble and push Lode vouch artifacts ==="
19	
20	test -f /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
21	  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt not found — step 01 must run first" >&2; exit 1; }
22	test -s /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
23	  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt is empty — nothing ensconced" >&2; exit 1; }
24

26	
27	# Ensure the shared buildx builder — shared library snippet (run once).
28	#@rbgjs_include buildx-bootstrap
29

31	  test -n "${STAMP}" || continue
32	
33	  ENVELOPE_FILE="/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
34	  test -f "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" \
35	    || { echo "FATAL: envelope not staged for ${STAMP}: ${ENVELOPE_FILE}" >&2; exit 1; }
36	
37	  VOUCH_URI="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}:${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}"
38	  echo "--- Vouch for ${STAMP} -> ${VOUCH_URI} ---"
39	
40	  CTX="/workspace/vouch_ctx_${STAMP}"
41	  mkdir -p "${CTX}"
42	  cp "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" "${CTX}/vouch.json"
43	  echo "FROM scratch"        >  "${CTX}/Dockerfile"
44	  echo "COPY vouch.json /"   >> "${CTX}/Dockerfile"
45	
46	  # Push the FROM-scratch vouch context — shared library snippet.

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 02: Push each Lode's provenance envelope as its :rbi_vouch artifact
# Builder: docker (from reliquary)
# Entrypoint: bash
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH
#
# Note: The Dockerfile echo-sequence below is intentional — this script runs
# inside a Cloud Build container, not under BCG module discipline.
#
# Step 01 (skopeo) staged one envelope per captured Lode at
# /workspace/lode_<stamp>_vouch.json and listed the stamps in
# /workspace/lode_stamps.txt. Each envelope rides into the SAME package as the
# base manifest, under the :rbi_vouch tag — a distinct manifest, one per Lode.
# Vouch content is architecture-independent; single-platform is sufficient.

set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Assemble and push Lode vouch artifacts ==="

test -f /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt not found — step 01 must run first" >&2; exit 1; }
test -s /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt is empty — nothing ensconced" >&2; exit 1; }

test -n "${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH missing" >&2; exit 1; }

# Ensure the shared buildx builder — shared library snippet (run once).
#@rbgjs_include buildx-bootstrap

while IFS= read -r STAMP || test -n "${STAMP}"; do
  test -n "${STAMP}" || continue

  ENVELOPE_FILE="/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
  test -f "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: envelope not staged for ${STAMP}: ${ENVELOPE_FILE}" >&2; exit 1; }

  VOUCH_URI="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}:${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}"
  echo "--- Vouch for ${STAMP} -> ${VOUCH_URI} ---"

  CTX="/workspace/vouch_ctx_${STAMP}"
  mkdir -p "${CTX}"
  cp "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" "${CTX}/vouch.json"
  echo "FROM scratch"        >  "${CTX}/Dockerfile"
  echo "COPY vouch.json /"   >> "${CTX}/Dockerfile"

  # Push the FROM-scratch vouch context — shared library snippet.
  PUSH_URI="${VOUCH_URI}"
  PUSH_PLATFORMS="linux/amd64"
  PUSH_CTX="${CTX}"
#@rbgjs_include buildx-push

  echo "Vouch pushed: ${VOUCH_URI}"
done < /workspace/lode_stamps.txt

echo "=== Vouch push step complete ==="

lastPrompt: Do you see a programmatic way to do this rather than comments which require human/llm interpretation?  Is it easy to make this safe with a little more live code?  I'm asking, I don't know.

content.text:
Yes — and your instinct is pointing at the better design. But the answer splits by channel, and the split is the whole story. I read the two live bole steps (`rbgjl01`, `rbgjl02`) to ground this against real code rather than the contract's intent.

## There are two channels, and they derive very differently

**1. The substitution channel (`_RBGL_*`) — fully programmatic, and strictly safer than a comment.**

A step's substitution-requires *is* the set of `_RBGL_*` names it references. That's not a declaration to be trusted — it's recoverable by scanning the (already-expanded) step body:

```
requires(step)  = grep -oE '_RBGL_[A-Z0-9_]+'  over non-comment lines
satisfied       = jq -r 'keys[]'  of the subs blob
fail if         requires(step) ⊄ satisfied   →  buc_die "step <id> reads <key>, not in substitutions"
```

I checked it against the live recipe: step 01 references 14 `_RBGL_*` keys, step 02 references 4; the bole subs blob defines 15; every reference is covered. Accept-path passes untouched. A typo (`_RBGL_TAG_VOCH`) or a renamed blob key fails instantly, at dispatch, naming the step and the key. This is *live code that cannot lie* — there's no comment to drift from the code, because the code's own variable references are the source of truth. ~20 lines.

And a nice simplification falls out: substitutions are automapped into **every** step globally, so a step never "provides" a substitution to a later step. The contract's "walk in order, accumulate provides" machinery is **entirely a /workspace concept** — the substitution check is just flat coverage, no ordering.

**2. The /workspace channel (inter-step files) — *not* safely static-derivable, and already self-guarding.**

The live steps do hand off files (`step 01` writes `/workspace/lode_stamps.txt` + `/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json`; `step 02` reads them). But deriving that statically is exactly the unsafe part:
- the paths are **templated by runtime loop vars** (`lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json`) — there's no literal to match across steps;
- **read-vs-write classification** in arbitrary bash (`>` vs `<` vs `test -f` vs `cp $var`) is the classic thing a static scanner gets wrong.

Crucially — **these steps already guard themselves at runtime**: `rbgjl02` opens with `test -f /workspace/lode_stamps.txt || FATAL "step 01 must run first"`. So the /workspace channel is already crash-fast *in the build*. A dispatch-time check would only pull that forward, and it can't be derived safely.

## So the clean story

There's a three-layer crash-fast picture already, and only one layer is both un-guarded *and* safely-derivable:

| Channel | Guarded today? | Safely derivable? |
|---|---|---|
| Snippet shell-vars (`TOKEN`,`ORIGIN`…) | yes — inside snippets | n/a (step-internal) |
| /workspace inter-step files | yes — `test -f … \|\| FATAL` in-step | **no** (templated paths, read/write ambiguity) |
| Substitution `_RBGL_*` | **no** — empty-expands silently | **yes** (scan references) |

The validator should target precisely that bottom row: pull the substitution-coverage check forward to dispatch, by **derivation, not declaration**. No comments anywhere.

## The one decision for you

This narrows the validator from the RBSCJ contract's *two declared channels* to *one derived channel* — so it's a contract revision I'd flag, not route around silently (§"Requires/provides format" would change from "each step declares two token lists" to "substitution coverage is derived; /workspace relies on the steps' own in-build guards").

- **(A) Derive substitutions only; defer /workspace.** Validator is a pure programmatic scan, zero trust. The /workspace channel keeps its existing in-step runtime guards; we add a dispatch-time /workspace mechanism only when a future kind actually needs one. The paddock premise — "generalize against instances we have, never stub the ones we don't" — actively backs *not* building /workspace declaration machinery speculatively now. **My recommendation.**
- **(B) Hybrid.** Same derived substitution scan, *plus* a declared `/workspace` requires/provides (declarations only where derivation is unsafe). More faithful to the written contract, but reintroduces the drift-prone declaration for the one channel that's already self-guarding in-build.

Both fully satisfy the pace's Done. (A) is less code, no trust, and the safety is total because there's nothing to interpret. Which way do you want it — and want me to mount it that way?

Locked: **(A)** — derive substitutions, defer /workspace. Proceeding.

Let me start with the decided, unambiguous core — the validator in the spine — then settle where its unit tests live, then shellcheck and the RBSCJ refresh.

First, the gate function and its wiring into `zrbld_spine_dispatch`:

input.old_string:
######################################################################
# Capture-assembly spine (zrbld_spine_*)

# Internal: compose, submit, and poll a Lode capture Cloud Build from a recipe
input.new_string:
######################################################################
# Capture-assembly spine (zrbld_spine_*)

# Internal: dispatch-time substitution-coverage gate. Each capture step reads
# automapped _RBGL_* substitution registers; a reference to a key the body's
# substitutions blob does not define expands to empty inside the build and
# corrupts the capture silently — the one composition fault that is neither
# guarded in-step (unlike the /workspace handoff, which the steps test -f for
# themselves) nor cheap to surface (it costs the whole cook). This derives the
# step's substitution-requires by scanning its include-expanded body for _RBGL_*
# references on non-comment lines — the references ARE the requires, with no
# declaration to drift from the code — and fails dispatch before submit if any
# referenced key is absent from the blob.
#
# Substitution-only by design (RBSCJ "Requires/provides format"): substitutions
# automap into every step, so coverage is flat — no ordering, no cross-step
# provides. The /workspace inter-step channel keeps its own in-step runtime
# guards and is out of scope here.
#
# Args: step_id subs_keys_file expanded_body_file
zrbld_spine_validate() {
  local -r z_step_id="${1:?Step id required}"
  local -r z_keys_file="${2:?Substitution keys file required}"
  local -r z_body_file="${3:?Expanded body file required}"

  local -r z_refs_file="${z_body_file}.refs"
  # References on non-comment lines only — a _RBGL_ token in a leading-# comment
  # is documentation, not a read, and must not force coverage. (A trailing inline
  # comment is not stripped; a doc mention there conservatively requires the key,
  # which the blob supplies anyway.) grep -o yields 1 on no match, so the pipeline
  # tolerates an empty reference set — a step may legitimately read no register.
  grep -vE '^[[:space:]]*#' "${z_body_file}" \
    | grep -oE '_RBGL_[A-Z0-9_]+' \
    | sort -u > "${z_refs_file}" || true

  local z_ref=""
  while IFS= read -r z_ref; do
    test -n "${z_ref}" || continue
    grep -qxF "${z_ref}" "${z_keys_file}" \
      || buc_die "Recipe step '${z_step_id}' reads substitution ${z_ref}, absent from the composition's substitutions blob"
  done < "${z_refs_file}"
}

# Internal: compose, submit, and poll a Lode capture Cloud Build from a recipe

# Internal: compose, submit, and poll a Lode capture Cloud Build from a recipe
toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Lode - capture-assembly spine (guard-free cluster, sourced by
# rbldk_): the data-driven Cloud Build composer shared by every Lode capture
# kind. Takes a recipe (ordered, pre-resolved step rows) plus an opaque
# substitutions blob, composes the Build resource, submits, polls, and decodes a
# step's buildStepOutputs slot. Owns NO kind knowledge: the recipe and the
# substitutions are data; the only kind-flavored build knob (the poll ceiling) is
# passed by the body. Capture-domain constants (the mason capture SA, the TETHER
# pool, the regime build timeout) are spine-owned, because every Lode capture
# fetches upstream bytes as mason over the network — that is what capture is, not
# a per-kind choice. Built on the rbfcb_ build primitives (write-script-body,
# wait-build-completion), which it calls rather than absorbs.
#
# Contract: RBSCJ "Capture Composition Contract".

set -euo pipefail

######################################################################
# Capture-assembly spine (zrbld_spine_*)

# Internal: compose, submit, and poll a Lode capture Cloud Build from a recipe
# plus an opaque substitutions blob.
#
# A recipe row is a |-delimited 4-tuple: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint
#   - script_path:   absolute path to the step script, pre-resolved by the body
#                    (the spine owns no steps-directory knowledge)
#   - builder_image: the step's builder image ref (colons allowed — hence the
#                    | delimiter, since image tags/digests contain colons)
#   - id:            the Cloud Build step id
#   - entrypoint:    bash | sh | python3 — selects the composed shebang line
#
# The substitutions file holds a JSON object the spine slots verbatim into the
# Build envelope's `substitutions` field; the spine reads no key from it. The
# envelope shape (serviceAccount, options.automapSubstitutions, options.logging,
# options.pool, timeout) is the spine's; the values that vary by capture domain
# (mason SA, TETHER pool, regime timeout) are spine-owned constants.
#
# Args: token label poll_ceiling subs_file temp_prefix recipe_row...
zrbld_spine_dispatch() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}";                  shift
  local -r z_label="${1:?Label required}";                  shift
  local -r z_poll_ceiling="${1:?Poll ceiling required}";    shift
  local -r z_subs_file="${1:?Substitutions file required}"; shift
  local -r z_temp_prefix="${1:?Temp prefix required}";      shift
  test "$#" -ge 1 || buc_die "zrbld_spine_dispatch: recipe requires at least one step row"

  test -s "${z_subs_file}" || buc_die "Substitutions file missing or empty: ${z_subs_file}"

  buc_step "Composing ${z_label} Cloud Build steps from recipe"
  local -r z_steps_file="${z_temp_prefix}steps.json"
  echo "[]" > "${z_steps_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize ${z_label} steps JSON"

  local z_row=""
  local z_script_path=""
  local z_builder=""
  local z_id=""
  local z_entrypoint=""
  local z_body_file=""
  local z_escaped_file=""
  local z_steps_built=""
  local z_body=""
  local z_shebang=""
  for z_row in "$@"; do
    IFS='|' read -r z_script_path z_builder z_id z_entrypoint <<<"${z_row}"
    test -n "${z_script_path}" || buc_die "Recipe row missing script_path: ${z_row}"
    test -n "${z_builder}"     || buc_die "Recipe row missing builder_image: ${z_row}"
    test -n "${z_id}"          || buc_die "Recipe row missing id: ${z_row}"
    test -f "${z_script_path}" || buc_die "Step script not found: ${z_script_path}"

    z_body_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_id}_body.txt"
    z_escaped_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_id}_escaped.txt"
    z_steps_built="${z_temp_prefix}${z_id}_steps.json"

    zrbfc_write_script_body "${z_script_path}" "${z_body_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to read step script: ${z_script_path}"
    zrbfc_expand_includes "${z_body_file}" "${ZRBFC_RBGJS_SNIPPETS_DIR}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to expand snippet includes in step: ${z_script_path}"
    z_body=$(<"${z_body_file}")
    test -n "${z_body}" || buc_die "Empty step script body: ${z_script_path}"

    case "${z_entrypoint}" in
      bash)    z_shebang="#!/bin/bash" ;;
      sh)      z_shebang="#!/bin/sh" ;;
      python3) z_shebang="#!/usr/bin/env python3" ;;
      *)       buc_die "Unknown entrypoint '${z_entrypoint}' in recipe row: ${z_row}" ;;
    esac
    printf '%s\n%s' "${z_shebang}" "${z_body}" > "${z_escaped_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write escaped step body for ${z_id}"

    jq \
      --arg name "${z_builder}" \
      --arg id "${z_id}" \
      --rawfile script "${z_escaped_file}" \
      '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \
      "${z_steps_file}" > "${z_steps_built}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to append step ${z_id}"
    mv "${z_steps_built}" "${z_steps_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to update steps JSON for ${z_id}"
  done

  buc_log_args "Composing ${z_label} Build resource JSON"
  local -r z_build_file="${z_temp_prefix}build.json"
  local -r z_mason_sa="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}"

  jq -n \
    --slurpfile zjq_steps   "${z_steps_file}" \
    --slurpfile zjq_subs    "${z_subs_file}" \
    --arg       zjq_sa      "${z_mason_sa}" \
    --arg       zjq_pool    "${RBDC_POOL_TETHER}" \
    --arg       zjq_timeout "${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT}" \
    '{
      steps: $zjq_steps[0],
      substitutions: $zjq_subs[0],
      serviceAccount: $zjq_sa,
      options: {
        automapSubstitutions: true,
        logging: "CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY",
        pool: { name: $zjq_pool }
      },
      timeout: $zjq_timeout
    }' > "${z_build_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to compose ${z_label} build JSON"

  buc_log_args "${z_label} build JSON: ${z_build_file}"

  rbrd_check "${z_token}"

  buc_step "Submitting ${z_label} Cloud Build"
  rbuh_json "POST" "${ZRBFC_GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL}" "${z_token}" \
    "lode_build_create" "${z_build_file}"
  rbuh_require_ok "${z_label} build submission" "lode_build_create"

  local z_build_id
  z_build_id=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "lode_build_create" '.metadata.build.id') \
    || buc_die "Failed to capture build ID from builds.create response"
  test -n "${z_build_id}" || buc_die "Build ID empty in builds.create response"
  echo "${z_build_id}" > "${ZRBFC_BUILD_ID_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to persist build ID"

  local -r z_console_url="${ZRBFC_CLOUD_QUERY_BASE};region=${RBGD_GCB_REGION}/${z_build_id}?project=${RBGD_GCB_PROJECT_ID}"
  buc_info "${z_label} Cloud Build submitted: ${z_build_id}"
  buc_link "Click to " "Open build in Cloud Console" "${z_console_url}"

  zrbfc_wait_build_completion "${z_poll_ceiling}" "${z_label}"
}

# Internal: decode the base64 JSON payload a step wrote to its buildStepOutputs
# slot into a destination file. The step index is the only generic parameter;
# what the decoded JSON means (its member-envelope shape) is the body's
# knowledge, not the spine's. Reads ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE (the terminal build
# result registered by zrbfc_wait_build_completion).
# Args: step_index dest_file
zrbld_spine_extract() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  local -r z_step_index="${1:?Step index required}"
  local -r z_dest_file="${2:?Destination file required}"
  local -r z_b64_file="${z_dest_file}.b64"

  jq -r ".results.buildStepOutputs[${z_step_index}] // empty" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE}" \
    > "${z_b64_file}" || buc_die "Failed to extract buildStepOutputs[${z_step_index}] from build result"
  test -s "${z_b64_file}" || buc_die "No buildStepOutputs[${z_step_index}] in build result — step produced no output"

  rbgo_base64_decode_file_to_file "${z_b64_file}" "${z_dest_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to decode buildStepOutputs[${z_step_index}] base64"
  test -s "${z_dest_file}" || buc_die "Empty decoded buildStepOutputs[${z_step_index}]"
}

# eof

content.text: Now extract the key set once, before the composition loop:

input.old_string:
test -s "${z_subs_file}" || buc_die "Substitutions file missing or empty: ${z_subs_file}"

  buc_step "Composing ${z_label} Cloud Build steps from recipe"
input.new_string:
test -s "${z_subs_file}" || buc_die "Substitutions file missing or empty: ${z_subs_file}"

  # Read the substitutions blob's keys once for the dispatch-time coverage gate;
  # the per-step scan rides the composition loop below, where each step body is
  # already include-expanded (zrbld_spine_validate).
  local -r z_keys_file="${z_temp_prefix}subs_keys.txt"
  jq -r 'keys[]' "${z_subs_file}" | sort -u > "${z_keys_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to read substitution keys from ${z_subs_file}"

  buc_step "Composing ${z_label} Cloud Build steps from recipe"

content.text: The core gate is in place. Now I need to find where bash-function unit tests live in this repo, since the validator is bash (not Rust theurge). Let me investigate the test substrate.

input.description: Map bash-function unit test infra
input.prompt:
I need to find how bash functions in the `Tools/rbk/` directory are unit-tested in this repo (project root: /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster).

Context: there is a Rust-based "theurge" test orchestrator at `Tools/rbk/rbtd/` that runs crucible/fixture tests. The test suites include fixtures like "enrollment-validation", "regime-validation", "regime-smoke" (the "fast" suite, no external dependencies). I need to determine whether these fast-suite validation fixtures are (a) Rust unit tests inside the theurge crate, (b) standalone bash test scripts that theurge shells out to, or (c) something else — and CRITICALLY, what the established pattern is for unit-testing a *pure bash function* that sources an rbk module and asserts on its behavior (e.g. a function that should call `buc_die` on bad input and succeed on good input).

Please determine, with concrete file paths and short excerpts:

1. What "enrollment-validation" and "regime-validation" fixtures actually ARE — find their definition. Are they `.rs` files, `.sh` files, or data files? Where do they live? How does theurge discover and run a fixture by name (e.g. `tt/rbw-tf.FixtureRun.sh regime-validation`)?

2. Is there ANY existing pattern in the repo where a bash function from an rbk `.sh` module is exercised by a test that sources the module, calls the function with crafted inputs, and asserts it dies (non-zero / buc_die) on bad input vs succeeds on good input? Search for test files that `source` rbk modules. Look at the BUK test framework (`Tools/buk/but_test.sh`, `Tools/buk/butt_testbench.sh`) and any `*_testbench.sh` or fixture scripts. Show me how a "this should fail" assertion is expressed.

3. Specifically: is there a way fixtures assert that a bash invocation FAILS with a specific error (a reject-path / negative test)? The validation fixtures (enrollment-validation has 47 cases, regime-validation 21) sound like they test rejection of bad config — show me exactly how one negative case is written, with the file and a representative excerpt.

4. Where would a NEW unit test for a function in `Tools/rbk/rblds_Spine.sh` (a function named `zrbld_spine_validate` that takes a step_id, a keys-file path, and a body-file path, and calls `buc_die` if the body references a `_RBGL_*` token not present in the keys file) most naturally live, following the established convention? Identify the exact directory/file pattern and how it would be registered/discovered and run via a `tt/` tabtarget.

Report the concrete mechanism (file naming, registration, how to run a single new test) so I can author a reject-path + accept-path test that fits the existing convention. Be thorough — read the actual fixture-running code in theurge and the BUK test framework. Do NOT write any code; just report findings with paths and excerpts.

content.text: The convention is clear: Rust test cases in theurge that source the bash module and assert exit codes (the regime-validation pattern). Let me read the actual code to get the exact APIs right before authoring — I won't trust a paraphrase for compilation-critical detail.

91	        Self { label, setup, command: "buv_vet \"TEST\"", expect_ok: true }
92	    }
93	    const fn fatal(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str) -> Self {
94	        Self { label, setup, command: "buv_vet \"TEST\"", expect_ok: false }
95	    }
96	    const fn ok_cmd(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str, command: &'static str) -> Self {
97	        Self { label, setup, command, expect_ok: true }
98	    }

100	        Self { label, setup, command, expect_ok: false }
101	    }
102	}

104	/// Run a bash script, return (exit_code, stdout, stderr). Saves traces to case dir.
105	fn rbtdrf_run_bash(
106	    project_root: &Path,
107	    script: &str,
108	    dir: &Path,
109	    trace_prefix: &str,
110	) -> Result<(i32, String, String), String> {
111	    let output = Command::new(rbtdri_bash_program())
112	        .arg("-c")
113	        .arg(script)
114	        .current_dir(project_root)
115	        .output()
116	        .map_err(|e| format!("bash execution failed: {}", e))?;
117	
118	    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).into_owned();
119	    let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).into_owned();
120	    let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);
121	
122	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-script.sh", trace_prefix)), script);
123	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stdout.txt", trace_prefix)), &stdout);
124	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stderr.txt", trace_prefix)), &stderr);
125	
126	    Ok((code, stdout, stderr))
127	}
